what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry that summarizes a claude-ds analysis note about a third pitch-deck ingest folder ('recent-pitches'), capturing its observations, standouts, conventions, questions, themes, novelty, and contribution to a fused design-language model.
observations: Records project metadata (claude-ds), source path, model (opus), and a detailed 'what_it_does' statement.
Lists eight observations covering the central verdict, measured values, ratio/density independence, slide-as-template proof, new atoms/patterns, 0-9 layer deltas, and resolution of an open question.
Highlights three standouts: template-equivalence via p-25/p-49, semantic bullet glyphs, and explicit resolution of 'density-perp-ratio?'.
Documents four conventions: 0-9 layer diff format, glyph classification, cross-references to sibling docs, and stress-test framing.
Includes an empty concerns list and a one-line notes summary.
Asks two questions about externally referenced archetypes and the physical ingest sample location.
Provides purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, and embedding summaries.
standouts: The central verdict that the design language is ratio-invariant and the LOD ladder gains a third high-detail rung.
Empirical slide-as-template proof using p-25 and p-49 with identical 'Our Entry Markets' layout but swapped data.
Semantic bullet-glyph distinction: gold triangle for plain lists and gold arrow for causal/flow lists.
Resolution of the AXES.md open question 'density-perp-ratio?' as independent.
conventions: Uses a 0-9 numbered-layer diff format shared with sibling analysis notes.
Uses checkmark/plus/pencil/warning glyphs to classify findings.
Cross-references sibling docs (SYSTEM-GAPS.md, AXES.md) rather than restating.
Frames findings as answers to pre-set stress-test questions.
notes: A ledger summary of a stress-test analysis note validating that the extracted design DNA holds across a new 3:2 aspect ratio and a higher detail level.
questions: What are the 13 archetypes / baseline atoms referenced as already established elsewhere?
Where is the ingest sample data physically located (ingest/recent-pitches/) and is it still present?
What are the exact labels and contents of the 0-9 numbered layers?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the ledger entry documents a stress-test analysis and reports stress-test results with measured evidence.
apparent_themes: Generative model where design = f(content, axis)
Invariance under surface parameters such as aspect ratio and density
Templates as fixed-layout plus swappable data
intention_signals: To confirm, with a deliberately different sample, that the extracted design DNA generalises across aspect ratios and detail levels, and to harvest new reusable atoms before synthesising the system.
novelty: The empirical template-equivalence proof (two slides, same layout, different data) and the semantic bullet-glyph distinction, alongside the ratio-invariance finding.
contribution: Contributes the ratio-invariance finding, the LOD-ladder third rung, the slide-as-template evidence, and a set of new design atoms (QR card, flow-bullet, quadrant map, team grid, geo locator, channel strip) to the fused design-language model.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger entry summarizing a claude-ds analysis note that a 3:2 high-detail pitch-deck folder confirms a ratio- and density-invariant design language, proves reusable data-swappable slide templates, and adds new design atoms.
intention_for_embedding: To capture validation results and harvested design atoms from a stress-test sample before synthesizing them into a generative design system.
